# Continuation — the-forge — generation 001
<!-- written: 2026-05-15T09:00:00Z · role: worker · prev: n/a -->

<!--
  Golden fixture for test/validate-continuation.sh. Synthesized from the shape
  of a real shipped temper continuation file with session-specific details
  redacted. The body of each section is intentionally short but non-empty so
  the validator's "section has content" rule has a real, passing example.
-->

## Hard constraints (RESTATED VERBATIM — do not summarize)

- Branch: `feat/#194-validate-continuation`. PR must include `closes #194`.
- Dev mode: `balanced`. Tests written after implementation; check command is
  the PR gate.
- Slice: `slice:logic`. No UI / no visual review.
- Acceptance: validator passes on the golden fixture and fails on each of:
  missing heading, empty section, fewer than five sections, sections in wrong
  order. Wired into `test/run-tests.sh`.
- Do not merge — stop at green CI; `/seal` ships the batch.

## Execution frontier

- **Branch:** `feat/#194-validate-continuation`
- **Open PR(s):** n/a (not yet pushed)
- **Last sentinel:** n/a (first generation)
- **Dispatch queue:** validator script written; companion `*.test.sh` next.
- **Mid-flight state:** none — about to add the `*.test.sh` that calls the
  validator with the golden fixture plus negative cases.

## Conversation summary

Sub-phase 3a slice 3: build a code-level check that a hardened continuation
file actually has its five mandatory sections, in order, each with a body.
The audit's most-repeated finding is that The Forge enforces contracts in
prose only; this slice is one of four cheap validators that close the gap
for the load-bearing artifacts. Format is pinned to
`templates/continuation-gen.md`; if the template grows a section, the
validator's `REQUIRED_SECTIONS` array grows with it.

## Next concrete action

Write `test/validate-continuation.test.sh`: source `lib/assert.sh`, invoke
the validator against this fixture (expect exit 0), then generate four
temp-file negative cases (missing heading, empty section, four sections,
sections-swapped) and assert exit 1 on each.

## Notes / scratch

The HTML-comment stripper in the validator is a small awk state machine
because the template ships with `<!-- ... -->` placeholder hints inside
section bodies — a naive "is the body empty after blanks?" check would
false-pass on a template that was never filled in.
